description: Machine-readable catalog of Cedar token pipeline Architecture Decision Records

adrs.title: Canonical Token Model
adrs.description: Defines the governed, platform-agnostic token shape. Includes the $extensions.cedar model for option token platform data, alias token platform references, and the SD pipeline constraint on {ref} resolution.
keyDecisions: Three-tier token architecture: Options → Alias → Semantic
Dot-delimited canonical paths (no hyphens, underscores, or slashes)
$extensions.cedar as the only location for non-DTCG data
Platform data on option tokens via $extensions.cedar.platformOverrides
Appearance variants via $extensions.cedar.appearances
Palette structure with inheritance from default

adrs.title: Token Normalization Layer
adrs.description: Defines how raw Figma files are transformed into the canonical model. Documents the four-file platform convention, the Figma input contract, and the governed invariants.
keyDecisions: Four-file platform convention: web-light, web-dark, ios-light, ios-dark
Schema-backed Figma-to-canonical path mapping in src/schema/token-schema.json
Build fails on unmapped Figma paths (designer rename guard)
Platform data merged in mergeColorVariants() during normalization
color.primitives NOT written to canonical output

adrs.title: Figma Input Contract
adrs.description: Documents the structure of raw Figma inputs and the schema-backed governance contract in src/schema/token-schema.json. Includes the four-file platform input contract and process for adding new platforms or appearances.
keyDecisions: Explicit path mapping in src/schema/token-schema.json (no string inference)
Four Figma platform files encode platform × appearance matrix
Appearance dimension encoded in filename, not file contents
Raw Figma Variables and Local Styles as dual input sources
Schema mapping requires design + engineering review to change

adrs.title: Semantic Token Architecture
adrs.description: Defines the three-tier token architecture (Options → Alias → Component). Updated with spike findings: color.option.* path convention, platform override governance rules.
keyDecisions: Three-tier model: Foundations (Options) → Alias → Component
Platform overrides vs new option token decision criteria
Semantic naming grammar across all token categories
TypeScript outputs organized by semantic category, not superficial type

adrs.title: Transform Layer & Platform Outputs
adrs.description: Defines how canonical tokens are transformed into CSS, iOS, Android, and other outputs, including the SD v5 normative pipeline constraints.
keyDecisions: SD v5 constraints: no {ref} in $extensions.cedar, action-owns-resolution pattern
Modular output organization by semantic category
Mode-specific type generation for TypeScript
Separate output files per mode/platform
Configuration Map as machine-readable index artifact

adrs.title: State Layer System
adrs.description: Defines interactive state tokens (hover, pressed, focus, disabled, selected, error) and the hybrid explicit/computed overlay approach.
keyDecisions: Hybrid approach: explicit state tokens with metadata for future computed overlays
State metadata: derivedFrom, stateType, overlayEquivalent
Focus uses dedicated indicator tokens, not color changes
Disabled uses opacity reduction, not separate colors
Selected is distinct semantic token, not state modifier

adrs.title: Modes and Palettes
adrs.description: Defines mode architecture and palette architecture. Updated with spike findings on how appearances actually work in the pipeline (option token appearances.dark vs Figma mode axis). Light/dark modes and default/sale palettes implemented.
keyDecisions: Environmental modes as single Figma mode axis (light, dark, high-contrast)
Platform differences as metadata, not modes
Palettes are surface-scoped, not page-level
Web: dynamic palette switching via CSS custom properties
Native: static palette variants via enums
Spike finding: appearances encoded on option tokens, not alias tokens

adrs.title: Responsive and Adaptive Tokens
adrs.description: Defines breakpoint tokens, fluid spacing, fluid typography, container query tokens, and density tokens. Fluid spacing with clamp() expressions implemented.
keyDecisions: Hybrid responsive system: fixed breakpoints + fluid tokens + container queries
Preserve existing Cedar breakpoints (xs, sm, md, lg) + add xl, xxl
Fluid spacing using clamp() with min/ideal/max structure
Container query breakpoints for component-level responsiveness
Density tokens as semantic multipliers for spacing

adrs.title: Accessibility Requirements
adrs.description: Embeds WCAG 2.2 Level AA requirements into the token system: focus indicators, touch targets, color contrast, high contrast mode, motion preferences.
keyDecisions: Accessibility-first tokens embedded in design system
Focus indicators: 2px minimum, 3:1 contrast (AAA)
Touch targets: 44×44px standard (AAA), 24×24px minimum (AA with spacing)
Color contrast metadata on tokens for automated validation
High contrast mode as mode variant with AAA contrast requirements

adrs.title: Token Documentation Architecture
adrs.description: Defines split-authority token docs architecture: Figma docs plus repo-owned governance metadata merged in canonical.
keyDecisions: Split authority: Figma for docs, repo for governance metadata
$extensions.cedar.docs for Figma descriptions
$extensions.cedar.governance for repo lifecycle/usage metadata
Option tokens can have Figma descriptions, semantic tokens cannot
TypeScript generation extracts docs.summary as JSDoc comments

adrs.title: Hybrid Alias Resolution
adrs.description: Adopts hybrid alias refs plus $extensions.cedar.resolved values with normalization input validation.
keyDecisions: Keep alias $value references unchanged as canonical source of truth
Add pre-resolved values in $extensions.cedar.resolved during normalization
Transforms/actions get deterministic value source without dictionary traversal
Normalization input validation against Figma Input Contract

adrs.title: Harmonic Interval Validation
adrs.description: Defines harmonic validation system with relationships section and harmonicInterval metadata for perceptual correctness checking.
keyDecisions: relationships is a distinct repo-authored section from usage pairingRules
harmonicInterval carries both deltaL and chromaEnvelope as named subsections
chromaEnvelope stores parabola parameters per token
siblingChromaRatio stored in harmonicInterval, not relationships
triadDeltas stored for all three pair directions
intervals field (WCAG) retained unchanged from harmonicInterval
intervalRule is foreign key into harmony-registry.json

adrs.title: Cedar Token Consumer Models
adrs.description: Defines every consumer model Cedar commits to supporting (web, iOS, Android, Tailwind, Sass, TypeScript), what Cedar produces for each, who owns the integration layer, and where Cedar's responsibility ends. Prerequisite to the token spike.
keyDecisions: Eight consumer models defined: Cedar components, CSS/vanilla web, Sass, TypeScript/JS, Tailwind CSS, iOS, Android, Figma
Layer 1 (token repository) produces platform-agnostic atomic values
Layer 2 (platform libraries) own composite expressions and integration adapters
Explicit out-of-scope list: React Native, CSS-in-JS, Web Components, W3C DTCG exports
Five open questions for the spike: fluid spacing on native, naming convention lock, rem-to-pt/dp conversion, palette mode on native, versioning policy

adrs.title: Composite Style Values
adrs.description: Defines what is allowed in the token repository (atomic single-value tokens) vs what is not (composite multi-property values like Sass mixins), and where composite styles should live instead (platform libraries).
keyDecisions: Token repository contains only atomic single-value tokens
Fluid/clamp values are atomic and allowed
Semantic alias tokens are allowed
Composite multi-property values (Sass mixins, CSS shorthand bundles) are not allowed in token repo
Figma text styles are decomposed into atomic tokens on export, not carried as composites
Composite styles live in platform-specific Cedar libraries (cedar-styles, iOS library, Android library, Tailwind preset)
Gap reporting is a first-class pipeline output

adrs.title: Cedar Tailwind Preset
adrs.description: Defines the Cedar-owned Tailwind preset (@rei/cedar-tailwind-preset) as the integration layer for Tailwind-based consumer teams. Preserves fluid spacing and palette mode runtime resolution via CSS custom properties.
keyDecisions: Cedar publishes and maintains @rei/cedar-tailwind-preset as official integration layer
Preset emits CSS custom properties at runtime (not static values) for spacing and color
All Cedar-sourced classes use cdr- prefix for clear boundary between design values and layout
Three-phase migration path: adopt preset, migrate to Cedar classes incrementally, enforce hard contract
Cedar commits to: preset maintenance alongside token releases, migration codemod, gap documentation, ESLint/Stylelint rule
Tailwind layout utilities (flex, grid, alignment) remain consumer's domain
